danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creature sprites in overworld and dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion
High-danger zones with puzzle and combat challenges

danger_signals.visual_form: Final dungeon with increasingly complex visual design
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate danger zone; requires virtue mastery to survive

danger_signals.visual_form: NPC interactions reflecting player moral failures
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of virtue status indicated through dialogue and NPC reactions

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for rest, commerce, and NPC interaction
Primary sanctuary and quest hub; represents legitimate authority and peace

safety_signals.visual_form: Dedicated meditation sites scattered across Britannia
safety_signals.meaning: Safe spiritual spaces for virtue enlightenment and reflection

safety_signals.visual_form: Hawkwind and other characters in non-hostile poses
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction points; feedback on moral progress

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Unified, prosperous settlements resembling Renaissance Italy; peaceful geography after Triad of Evil defeated

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Eight Virtues as spiritual identity markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Character development through virtue shrines; NPC recognition based on moral standing

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic dungeon monsters vs. overworld threats
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Concentrated in dungeons and wilderness; absent from civilized areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Prosperous, unified Britannia with stable cities and restored geography; Renaissance-like architecture and peaceful overworld
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs pursue normal lives; no panic or emergency dialogue; focus on spiritual guidance rather than survival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shrine accessibility changes based on rune possession; NPC reactions shift based on virtue levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hawkwind provides escalating feedback; NPCs respond differently to high vs. low virtue characters; bragging lowers Humility visibly in interactions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dialogue options reflect vice instead of virtue; NPC responses become dismissive or hostile
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lost virtue prevents shrine access; character distances from Avatar path; affected NPC dialogue and world reception

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stygian Abyss becomes accessible only with sufficient virtue mastery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final dungeon entry conditional on spiritual readiness; visual complexity increases as enlightenment deepens

environmental_storytelling.example: Visual evidence of world upheaval: continents risen/sank, new cities built to replace lost ones; establishes post-apocalyptic recovery narrative
Renaissance Italy/Camelot-inspired architecture communicates peace and cultural flourishing; contrasts with dark age dungeons
Eight scattered shrines each dedicated to a virtue; physical locations teach spiritual values through meditation mechanics
Stygian Abyss visual complexity and inaccessibility until virtue requirements met; depth communicates challenge escalation
Player profession (Bard, Paladin, Tinker, etc.) visually manifests highest-priority virtue choice from tarot dilemmas

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Provides encouraging feedback, advises shrine visits, suggests actions for virtue improvement
Chastises character, indicates distance from Avatar status, offers corrective guidance
Responsive to dialogue, offer quests, show respect for moral standing
Dismissive or hostile responses; bragging interaction lowers Humility and affects reception
Hostile sprite movement; require combat or evasion; no negotiation possible
Cannot access corresponding shrine; loses enlightenment progress; NPC dialogue reflects moral failure
Mantra repetition at shrines triggers visual/mechanical elevation; character status improves in world perception

notes: Ultima IV pioneered virtue-based character development and moral consequence visualization in RPGs. The game's world-state communication centers on ethical alignment rather than traditional good vs. evil factions. Visual manifestation of morality occurs through NPC reaction changes, shrine accessibility, and character acceptance in communities. The Renaissance-inspired prosperous setting contrasts sharply with dark dungeon interiors, spatially encoding safety vs. danger. Player actions directly affect world state perception: fleeing battle lowers Valor, bragging lowers Humility, alms-giving raises Sacrifice. Virtue is communicated through meditation mechanics, rune collection, and Hawkwind's feedback system—a proto-alignment system that visualizes spiritual progress through dialogue and environmental gating.
